risks: VMSS Flexible orchestration with Rolling upgrade policy requires Application Health Extension, which is not included in either VMSS resource — rolling upgrades will not function without it
SQL Managed Instance has zoneRedundant set to false — the data tier is not zone-redundant despite presentation and application tiers being distributed across zones 1, 2, and 3
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, Application Insights) are included — operational visibility into the infrastructure will be limited
No Key Vault resource for secure storage of SQL MI administrator credentials or VMSS SSH keys — secrets management relies on external configuration
No autoscale configuration for the application-tier VMSS (vmss-application-prod-001) — only the presentation tier has autoscale rules
Presentation-tier NSG allows HTTP/HTTPS inbound from any source (*) with no WAF or DDoS protection layer in front of the load balancer
correctionsRecommended: Add Application Health Extension to both VMSS virtual machine profiles to satisfy the Rolling upgrade policy requirement on Flexible orchestration
Add a Log Analytics workspace and enable diagnostic settings on the Load Balancer, NSGs, and SQL Managed Instance for centralized monitoring
Add an Azure Key Vault resource to store SQL MI admin password and VMSS SSH private keys, and reference it from the relevant resources
Add an autoscale settings resource (Microsoft.Insights/autoscaleSettings) targeting the application-tier VMSS for elastic scaling
Evaluate enabling zone redundancy on SQL MI (requires Business Critical tier) or explicitly document the single-zone risk as an accepted trade-off
Consider upgrading from L4 Load Balancer to Application Gateway with WAF v2 for layer-7 protection, or add Azure DDoS Protection Plan for the VNet
